methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $160M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Quantum-Systems is a Series C defense and security frontier-tech company founded by Florian Seibel, currently valued at €3B with $160M+ raised to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 10 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies, supplemented by 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Saronic, and Scale AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $160M total raised proxy and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yields $272M. Peer-median methodology across the 10-company comparable set—with a range spanning $500M to $9.2B—implies $1.4B, reflecting material variance driven by stage dispersion within the cohort (Series B entries at $1.4–$1.5B; Series D peers at $4.5–$9.2B).

The triangulated valuation range supports $231M (low), $824M (median), and $1.6B (high). The wide dispersion reflects two key uncertainties: first, the stage heterogeneity of the peer set itself, with Series D comparables (Saronic at $9.2B, Chaos Industries at $4.5B) materially exceeding median; second, Quantum-Systems' precise operational positioning relative to earlier-stage ($1.4–$1.5B) versus advanced-stage peers. A valuation in the $800M–$1.2B range appears supported by mid-stage and Series C peer clustering, though progression toward Series D could justify movement toward the upper bound.
